dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 6551 IH: New Bank Application Numbers Knowledge Act of 2025
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Loudermilk introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Financial Services

form.official-title: To require annual reports on national bank and Federal savings association charter applications, depository institution holding company applications, Federal deposit insurance applications, and State depository institution charter applications, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the New Bank Application Numbers Knowledge Act of 2025 or the New BANK Act of 2025.

section.header: Annual report on national bank and Federal savings association charter applications
section.text: The Comptroller of the Currency shall publish an annual report that includes—

paragraph.text: the number of national bank and Federal savings association charter applications received, approved on a preliminary basis, approved on a final basis, denied, withdrawn, expired, mooted, or returned;
the mean and median times for preliminary approval of such applications;
the mean and median times for final approval of such applications; and
common reasons leading to denial, withdrawal, or expiration of preliminary approval of such applications.

section.header: Annual report on Federal credit union charter applications
section.text: The National Credit Union Administration Board shall publish an annual report that includes—

paragraph.text: the number of Federal credit union charter applications received, proofs of concept established, approved on a final basis, denied, withdrawn, expired, mooted, or returned;
the mean and median times to establish proof of concept for such applications;
the mean and median times for final approval of such applications; and
common reasons leading to denial, withdrawal, or expiration of establishment of proof of concept of such applications.

section.header: Annual report on depository institution holding company applications
section.text: The Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System shall publish an annual report that includes—

paragraph.text: the number of applications to become a top-tier depository institution holding company received, approved, denied, withdrawn, mooted, or returned;
the mean and median times to approve such applications; and
common reasons leading to denial or withdrawal of such applications.

section.header: Annual report on Federal deposit insurance applications
section.text: The Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation shall publish an annual report that includes—

paragraph.text: the number of applications for deposit insurance received, approved, denied, withdrawn, mooted, or returned;
the mean and median times to approve such applications; and
common reasons leading to denial or withdrawal of such applications.

section.header: Annual report on State depository institution and State credit union charter applications

subsection.text: The Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System, the Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation, and the National Credit Union Administration Board shall, jointly, and in consultation with State banking regulators and State credit union regulators, publish an annual report that includes—

paragraph.text: the number of State depository institution charter applications received, approved, denied, withdrawn, or otherwise dispositioned by State banking regulators and State credit union regulators, with numbers for each State shown separately;
the mean and median times to approve such applications, with times for each State shown separately; and
common reasons leading to denial or withdrawal of such applications.
The term State means any State of the United States, the District of Columbia, and any territory of the United States.
The term State bank means any bank, banking association, trust company, savings bank, industrial bank, or other banking institution incorporated under the laws of any State.
The term State depository institution means—

subparagraph.text: a State bank or a State savings association; and
a State credit union, as defined in section 101 of the Federal Credit Union Act (12 U.S.C. 1752).

paragraph.text: The term State savings association means—

subparagraph.text: any building and loan association, savings and loan association, or homestead association incorporated under the laws of any State; and
any cooperative bank incorporated under the laws of any State that is not a State bank.
